description: Conformance vectors for the authorization_ref field in the action_ref preimage. authorization_ref = SHA-256(JCS(policy_snapshot_P)) captures the policy state at execution time. A policy update to P' does not invalidate a prior action_ref — it proves which policy was active. Verifiers MUST recompute authorization_ref from the presented policy snapshot and reject if it diverges from the embedded value.
action_ref_definition: action_ref = "sha256:" + hex(SHA-256(JCS({action_type, agent_id, authorization_ref, scope, timestamp})))

vectors.name: snapshot_match
vectors.description: Action authorized under policy P, verified against the same policy P. The verifier recomputes authorization_ref = SHA-256(JCS(P)) and finds it matches the embedded value. action_ref is byte-identical. Result: PASS.
vectors.rationale: authorization_ref in the preimage equals SHA-256(JCS(policy_P)). The verifier recomputes over the presented policy snapshot and arrives at the same value. action_ref is therefore byte-identical to the issued reference.

vectors.name: snapshot_mismatch
vectors.description: Action was authorized under policy P (authorization_ref = SHA-256(JCS(P))), but someone attempts to verify presenting P' (updated policy) as the policy snapshot. The verifier recomputes authorization_ref from P' and gets a different hash. action_ref recomputed over the tampered preimage diverges from the issued action_ref. Result: FAIL.
vectors.rationale: The verifier MUST recompute authorization_ref from the presented policy snapshot. SHA-256(JCS(P')) != SHA-256(JCS(P)), so the preimage changes, and the recomputed action_ref diverges from the issued action_ref. The verifier MUST reject.

vectors.name: policy_updated_after_action
vectors.description: Action authorized and executed under policy P. Policy is subsequently updated to P'. A verifier operating under P' receives the original action_ref, the policy_snapshot_P as historical evidence, and the current policy P'. The verifier computes SHA-256(JCS(policy_snapshot_P)) and confirms it matches authorization_ref embedded in the issued action_ref — proving P was active at execution time. The policy update does not invalidate the action_ref. Result: PASS.

verification_steps.step_2: Confirm recomputed authorization_ref == embedded authorization_ref in issued_preimage — TRUE

vectors.rationale: authorization_ref binds the policy state at execution time into the action_ref preimage. A subsequent policy update changes P but not the historical authorization_ref hash. The verifier can independently confirm that P was active when the action_ref was issued. This is the core invariant: action_ref is a commitment to both the action and the policy under which it was authorized.
